mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that lowers discount rates, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signals, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising housing starts (HOUST) signal supply-side disruptions and demand imbalances, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails to materialize with Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows or 20d returns remain positive despite conflicting INDPRO/HOUST signals.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4.0%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals inflationary pressures, prompting Fed rate hikes and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns remain positive in 60%+ of triggered instances, or interaction effect IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2.0%) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 720k) indicates robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns remain negative in 60%+ of triggered instances, or interaction effect IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production (INDPRO < 100) during a housing market slowdown (HOUST < 1350) signals supply-side contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns remain positive in 60%+ of triggered instances, or interaction effect IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) paired with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.5%) signals successful monetary tightening, reducing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns remain positive in 60%+ of triggered instances, or interaction effect IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDP (GDPC1 > 2.5%) during a housing market boom (HOUST > 1400) signals strong demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns remain negative in 60%+ of triggered instances, or interaction effect IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.
